Title: Verify mobile menu trigger and visibility
Preconditions:
1. QA environment accessible at ${FRONT_URL} (from .env)
2. Test user logged in
3. Browser viewport set to mobile (375px width) or actual mobile device
Steps:
Step 1 - Set viewport to 375px width (iPhone): Page renders in mobile layout
Step 2 - Navigate to Account area: Account page loads in mobile layout
Step 3 - Locate the account menu trigger: Hamburger icon, "Menu" button, or similar mobile menu trigger is visible
Step 4 - Tap/click the menu trigger: Account menu opens (slide-out panel, dropdown, or full-screen overlay)
Step 5 - Verify all menu groups visible: Purchasing, User, Corporate groups displayed (Marketing hidden if empty)
Step 6 - Close the mobile menu: Menu closes, page content visible again
Expected Result:
- Mobile menu trigger clearly visible and accessible
- Menu opens/closes smoothly with animation
- All menu groups and items accessible in mobile view
- Menu does not obscure critical page elements when closed
PASS: Menu trigger visible, opens/closes correctly, all items accessible
FAIL: Menu trigger missing, menu doesn't open, items missing, or broken layout

Title: Verify mobile menu structure matches desktop groups
Preconditions:
1. QA environment accessible
2. Test user logged in
3. Mobile viewport (375px) or mobile device
4. Mobile account menu opened
Steps:
Step 1 - Open mobile account menu: Menu displays
Step 2 - Verify "Purchasing" group items: Contains: Dashboard, Orders, Lists, Saved for Later (same as desktop)
Step 3 - Verify "User" group items: Contains: Profile, Addresses, Change Password, Saved Credit Cards (same as desktop)
Step 4 - Verify "Corporate" group items: Contains: Company Info, Company Members (same as desktop)
Step 5 - Verify menu item count: Total 8 menu items + Corporate (same as desktop)
Step 6 - Verify item ordering within groups: Same priority ordering as desktop menu
Expected Result:
- Mobile menu structure mirrors desktop grouped structure
- Same items, same groups, same ordering
- No items missing or duplicated compared to desktop
PASS: Mobile menu matches desktop structure exactly
FAIL: Missing groups, missing items, or different ordering

Title: Verify mobile menu navigation routes
Preconditions:
1. QA environment accessible
2. Test user logged in
3. Mobile viewport (375px)
4. Mobile account menu opened
Steps:
Step 1 - Tap "Dashboard" in mobile menu: Dashboard page loads, menu closes (or stays), correct URL
Step 2 - Re-open menu, tap "Orders": Orders page loads, correct URL, no console errors
Step 3 - Re-open menu, tap "Lists": Lists page loads, correct URL
Step 4 - Re-open menu, tap "Saved for Later": Saved for Later page loads, correct URL
Step 5 - Re-open menu, tap "Profile": Profile page loads, correct URL
Step 6 - Re-open menu, tap "Addresses": Addresses page loads, correct URL
Step 7 - Re-open menu, tap "Change Password": Change Password page loads, correct URL
Step 8 - Re-open menu, tap "Saved Credit Cards": Saved Credit Cards page loads, correct URL
Step 9 - Verify no console errors after all navigations: Console clean, no JavaScript errors
Expected Result:
- All 8 menu items navigate to correct pages on mobile
- Pages load correctly in mobile layout
- Menu behavior after navigation is consistent (auto-close or stay open per design)
- No console errors
PASS: All navigation routes work on mobile, pages load correctly
FAIL: Any route fails, page doesn't load, or console errors

Title: Verify mobile menu touch interactions
Preconditions:
1. QA environment accessible
2. Test user logged in
3. Mobile viewport (375px) or actual touch device
4. Mobile account menu opened
Steps:
Step 1 - Tap a menu item: Item responds to tap with visual feedback (highlight/ripple)
Step 2 - Verify touch target size: All menu items have minimum 44x44px touch target (WCAG)
Step 3 - Test swipe to close (if applicable): Swipe gesture closes the menu (if slide-out panel)
Step 4 - Test tap outside menu to close: Tapping outside the menu area closes it
Step 5 - Test scroll within menu (if items overflow): Menu scrolls smoothly if content exceeds viewport height
Step 6 - Verify no accidental taps: Sufficient spacing between items prevents accidental navigation
Expected Result:
- Touch targets meet 44x44px minimum
- Tap feedback visible
- Menu dismissal gestures work (tap outside, swipe)
- Scrolling works if menu content overflows
- No accidental taps due to tight spacing
PASS: Touch targets adequate, gestures work, smooth scrolling
FAIL: Touch targets too small, gestures broken, or accidental taps

Title: Verify mobile menu across viewports (iPhone and Android)
Preconditions:
1. QA environment accessible
2. Test user logged in
Steps:
Step 1 - Test at 375px width (iPhone SE/Mini): Menu displays correctly, no overflow, all items accessible
Step 2 - Test at 390px width (iPhone 14/15/16): Menu displays correctly
Step 3 - Test at 428px width (iPhone 14/15/16 Pro Max): Menu displays correctly
Step 4 - Test at 360px width (Android small): Menu displays correctly
Step 5 - Test at 412px width (Android Pixel/Samsung): Menu displays correctly
Step 6 - Test at 768px width (iPad / tablet portrait): Menu transitions between mobile and desktop layout correctly
Step 7 - Verify landscape orientation (667px height at 375px width): Menu adapts to landscape, scrollable if needed
Expected Result:
- Mobile menu renders correctly across all common mobile viewports
- No horizontal overflow or cut-off content
- Text readable without zooming
- Layout adapts properly at tablet breakpoint
PASS: Menu renders correctly at all viewports, no layout breaks
FAIL: Layout broken, content cut off, or unusable at any viewport

Title: Verify mobile menu active state and current page indicator
Preconditions:
1. QA environment accessible
2. Test user logged in
3. Mobile viewport (375px)
Steps:
Step 1 - Navigate to Orders page on mobile: Orders page loads
Step 2 - Open mobile menu: "Orders" item shows active/selected state (highlight, bold, icon)
Step 3 - Navigate to Profile page: Profile page loads
Step 4 - Open mobile menu: "Profile" item shows active state, "Orders" no longer active
Step 5 - Deep link to ${FRONT_URL}/account/addresses on mobile: Addresses page loads
Step 6 - Open mobile menu: "Addresses" item shows active state
Expected Result:
- Active menu item clearly indicated in mobile menu
- Only one item active at a time
- Active state updates correctly after navigation
- Deep links also trigger correct active state
PASS: Active state works correctly on mobile, matches current page
FAIL: No active indicator, multiple active items, or incorrect state
